stories.title: Trump says US troop deaths ‘a shame’ but necessary to prevent Iranian nuke
stories.summary:
Trump says US troop deaths ‘a shame’ but necessary to prevent Iranian nuke. President Donald Trump said that the deaths of U.S. troops in the war with Iran were “a shame” but portrayed it as a necessary sacrifice to prevent Tehran from obtaining a nuclear weapon.

Trump said the two soldiers killed in Jordan on Friday gave their lives for a greater cause, speaking with the New York […].

Al Jazeera: Iran war live: US military completes ninth night of strikes on Iran. Al Jazeera reports that uS forces attack Iran for ninth consecutive night as one US soldier killed in northern Iraq.

stories.title: JD And Usha Vance Welcome First Vice Presidential Baby In Over 150 Years
stories.summary:
JD And Usha Vance Welcome First Vice Presidential Baby In Over 150 Years. Vice President JD Vance and second lady Usha Vance welcomed their fourth child on Sunday.

Alec Neel Vance was born Sunday morning at Walter Reed Medical Center.

“Usha and the baby are happy and healthy, and our kids are overjoyed to meet their little brother,” Vance said on Sunday. Alec Neel Vance joins older brothers ...

stories.title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in U.S.
stories.summary:
Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in U.S.. A famous Cuban dissident artist and musician, Luis Manuel Otero Alcantara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.

BBC: Cuban dissident Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara goes into exile in US.

BBC reports that he was arrested in 2021 during Cuba's largest anti-government protests in decades and spent five years ⁠in prison.

stories.title: 5.5 magnitude earthquake hits Peru's Andes region and kills at least 5 people
stories.summary:
5.5 magnitude earthquake hits Peru's Andes region and kills at least 5 people. A 5.5-magnitude earthquake shook the Andes region of Peru, killing at least five people, authorities said Sunday.

More than 20 people were injured and 300 have been displaced.

Al Jazeera: Twin earthquakes strike Peru killing at least five. Al Jazeera reports that peruvian authorities say the magnitude 5.1 and 3.7 quakes were in the mountainous Junin region east of Lima.

stories.title: A ferry carrying 116 people capsizes off Guyana, with 67 rescued as search continues
stories.summary:
A ferry carrying 116 people capsizes off Guyana, with 67 rescued as search continues. A ferry carrying 116 passengers and crew capsized in Guyana, with 67 people rescued so far, authorities said Sunday.

stories.title: Dems Poised To Pick Another Far-Leftist To Replace Platner
stories.summary:
Dems Poised To Pick Another Far-Leftist To Replace Platner. Former Maine Senate President Troy Jackson has all but secured the Democratic nomination in one of the nation’s most closely watched Senate races after dominating the party’s delegate selection process over the weekend, prompting his two strongest rivals to suspend their campaigns ahead of next week’s state convention.

Jackson won overwhelming victories across Maine’s county ... Fox News: Platner ally Troy Jackson locks down delegates as top rivals for Democratic Senate nomination drop out.

Fox News reports that troy Jackson locked up delegates after rivals Shenna Bellows and Nirav Shah dropped out of the race to replace Graham Platner against Susan Collins.

stories.title: Tate Brothers Slapped With 38 New Charges In Expanding Sex Trafficking Case
stories.summary:
Tate Brothers Slapped With 38 New Charges In Expanding Sex Trafficking Case. British prosecutors have issued 38 new criminal charges against Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan after the pair were arrested in Miami on Saturday by federal officials on rape charges and an outstanding warrant in the United Kingdom.

The additional charges pave the way for possible extradition proceedings across the pond as the pair now ... Breitbart: Andrew and Tristan Tate Arrested in Miami as UK Crown Prosecutors Seek Extradition.

Breitbart reports that social media influencer Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan were arrested in Miami as Britain seeks their extradition. The post Andrew and Tristan Tate Arrested in Miami as UK Crown Prosecutors Seek Extradition appeared first on Breitbart .

stories.title: Anika Wells says Telstra must 'face the music' as regulator begins investigation – video
stories.summary:
Anika Wells says Telstra must 'face the music' as regulator begins investigation – video. The communications minister, Anika Wells, says Telstra must 'face the music' and rebuild public trust after its nationwide outage, vowing that the government will hold the telco to account.

She says Telstra should provide an expedited process for customers seeking compensation.

The Australian Communications and Media Authority's deputy chair Adam Suckling says the regulator is investigating the outage. Suckling says under laws related to the triple zero system, Telstra can face civil penaltie.

links.headline: Anika Wells says Telstra must 'face the music' as regulator begins investigation – video
links.excerpt: The communications minister, Anika Wells, says Telstra must 'face the music' and rebuild public trust after its nationwide outage, vowing that the government will hold the telco to account. She says Telstra should provide an expedited process for customers seeking compensation. The Australian Communications and Media Authority's deputy chair Adam Suckling says the regulator is investigating the outage. Suckling says under laws related to the triple zero system, Telstra can face civil penaltie

stories.summary:
Smart glasses are deeply creepy. Why are celebrities like Kylie Jenner endorsing them?.

Meta touts safety features – but for women, the dangers of these recording devices are obvious Imagine if every time you left the house, you couldn’t be sure that the stranger you met at a bar – or even the person walking by you in the street – wasn’t secretly recording you.

It sounds like something out of a Black Mirror episode, but let’s face it, the era of wearable.

stories.title: ‘We noticed a login from a new device’: the message from fraudsters targeting your X account
stories.summary:
‘We noticed a login from a new device’: the message from fraudsters targeting your X account. They are out to steal your password to commit further fraud such as crypto scams or phishing attacks You have had an X account for years, since it was known as Twitter.

When an email arrives about a new login from a location nowhere near where you live, alarm bells begin to ring.

“We noticed a login to your account from a new device. Was this you?” the email asks.

C.

stories.title: Case filed vs Bacolod journalist alarms Palace media task force
stories.summary:
Case filed vs Bacolod journalist alarms Palace media task force.

BACOLOD CITY, NEGROS OCCIDENTAL, Philippines — The Presidential Task Force on Media Security (PTFoMS) has expressed concern over the unjust vexation charges filed against Bacolod-based journalist Marchel Espina, former SunStar Cebu editor in chief, over an opinion piece published by the community newspaper in December 2024.

Espina voluntarily surrendered to the Philippine National Police’s Criminal.

stories.title: Public urged to help trace polluters in Laguna de Bay
stories.summary:
Public urged to help trace polluters in Laguna de Bay. LUCENA CITY – The Laguna Lake Development Authority (LLDA) has appealed to the public to help identify the sources of pollution in Laguna de Bay, saying community reports are crucial in holding environmental violators accountable.

In a public advisory issued Sunday, July 19, the LLDA acknowledged the role of residents and local officials in reporting.

stories.title: Inday, ‘habagat’ leave P885M in farm losses across BARMM
stories.summary:
Inday, ‘habagat’ leave P885M in farm losses across BARMM.

COTABATO CITY, BARMM, Philippines — Damage to agriculture in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) caused by Super Typhoon Inday (international name: Bavi) and the enhanced southwest monsoon (“habagat”) has reached nearly P885 million, a regional disaster official said on Sunday.

stories.title: PNP tightens security as Uplift aid reaches millions of Filipinos
stories.summary:
PNP tightens security as Uplift aid reaches millions of Filipinos.

MANILA, Philippines — As millions of Filipinos receive government aid this week, the Philippine National Police (PNP) has ordered heightened security measures nationwide for the expanded Unified Package for Livelihood, Industry, Food and Transport (Uplift) program.

The Department of Social Welfare and Development said the program is the latest round of financial assistance for individuals.

stories.title: 2 more Army bases harassed in Maguindanao
stories.summary:
2 more Army bases harassed in Maguindanao. COTABATO CITY — Attacks against Philippine Army forces and installations in the Maguindanao provinces in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) continue as the region prepares for the historic Bangsamoro Parliamentary Elections (BPE) on September 14.

stories.summary: Apple and Google ordered to purge ‘nudify’ apps from App Stores. In letters sent to Apple and Google, San Francisco City Attorney David Chiu said that both companies have long been aware that they are hosting apps in violation of state law.

stories.summary:
Trump has normalized crypto. Is it the path to the next financial collapse? | Eduardo Porter.

Cheerleading by the president, who made $1.2bn last year off uninsured currency, does not bode well for US economy The scale of the graft is decidedly off the charts, but the revelation that Donald Trump raked in a personal fortune of $2.2bn during his first year in office should come as no surprise.
